Settings.Subtitle: Settings
Settings.Language.Label: Display language
Settings.Language.Hint: Changes take effect after a restart.
Settings.Theme.Label: App theme
Settings.Theme.Hint: Choose light, dark, or follow the system theme. Applied immediately.
Settings.Storage.Hint: Volatile memory leaves nothing on disk; the history and the key are fully gone when the app exits. Changes take effect after a restart.
Settings.Protection.Hint: In addition to DPAPI, you can protect the key with either a passphrase (Argon2id) or Windows Hello (two-factor). Changes apply immediately in this session, and authentication will be required from the next launch.
Settings.Hello.Hint: Protect the key with face / fingerprint / PIN. Enabling this replaces passphrase protection (they cannot be combined).
Settings.Hello.Unavailable: Windows Hello is not available on this PC.
Settings.Excluded.Hint: Copies from the processes listed here are not captured (applied immediately).
Settings.Mask.Hint: Detects password-like strings and suppresses their capture (false positives are possible; applied immediately).
Settings.StripTracking.Hint: When a copied URL is captured, tracking parameters (utm_, fbclid, and so on) are removed automatically, so pasting from ClipVault gives a clean URL. The system clipboard itself is left untouched (applied immediately).
Settings.Retention.Hint: Pinned entries are exempt from the retention limits. Changes take effect after a restart.
Settings.AutoStart.Hint: Starts ClipVault in the tray when you log on to Windows.
Settings.Panic.Hint: Erases all history and destroys the master key (crypto-erase). After this, all stored ciphertext becomes permanently unrecoverable.
Settings.Panic.Name: Run panic wipe
Settings.Panic.Button: Erase everything and destroy the key
Settings.Panic.Dialog.Title: Erase everything and destroy the key?
Settings.Panic.Dialog.Body: This erases all history and destroys the master key (crypto-erase). The action cannot be undone, all stored ciphertext becomes permanently unrecoverable, and the app exits when it completes.
Settings.Privacy.Pending: The selected storage mode applies after the next restart. The above reflects the current session.
Settings.Privacy.Network: Network: none (no internetClient capability, fully local)
Settings.Privacy.Guarantee.Volatile: Running in volatile memory — history and the encryption key are kept only in RAM and are never written to disk. Everything is gone when the app exits.
Settings.Privacy.Guarantee.DpapiOnly: Running on encrypted disk — data at rest is encrypted with ChaCha20-Poly1305 (with AAD). The key is sealed with Windows DPAPI (current user).
Settings.Privacy.Guarantee.Passphrase: Running on encrypted disk — encrypted with ChaCha20-Poly1305 (with AAD). The key is protected by DPAPI + a passphrase (Argon2id), two-factor.
Settings.Privacy.Guarantee.Hello: Running on encrypted disk — encrypted with ChaCha20-Poly1305 (with AAD). The key is protected by DPAPI + Windows Hello (TPM), two-factor.
Settings.Privacy.Extra.Volatile: No extra key protection is needed because no key is stored on disk. To keep history, switch Storage to encrypted disk.
Settings.Privacy.Extra.DpapiOnly: You can additionally enable a passphrase (Argon2id) or Windows Hello for two-factor protection (under Key protection).
Settings.Privacy.Extra.Passphrase: Passphrase two-factor protection is enabled. You can switch to Windows Hello instead.
Settings.Privacy.Extra.Hello: Windows Hello two-factor protection is enabled.
Settings.Msg.OperationFailed: The operation failed. Please try again later.
Settings.Msg.HelloFailedOrCancelled: Windows Hello authentication failed or was cancelled. Please try again.
Settings.Msg.PassphraseMismatch: The new passphrase and the confirmation do not match.
Settings.Msg.NeedCurrentToRemove: The current passphrase is required to remove protection.
Settings.Msg.PassphraseRemoved: Passphrase protection has been removed (DPAPI only).
Settings.Msg.NeedCurrentToSwitchHello: The current passphrase is required to switch to Windows Hello.
Settings.Msg.HelloProtected: Protected with Windows Hello. This change requires authentication from the next launch.
Settings.Msg.HelloDisabled: Windows Hello protection has been removed (DPAPI only). This change takes effect from the next launch.
Unlock.Passphrase.Description: This vault is protected by a passphrase. Enter the passphrase to continue.
Unlock.Passphrase.Wrong: The passphrase is incorrect. Please try again.
Unlock.Hello.Description: This vault is protected by Windows Hello. Authenticate with Windows Hello (face / fingerprint / PIN) to continue.
Unlock.Hello.Failed: Windows Hello authentication failed. Please try again.
History.ImageRenderFailed: The image could not be displayed.
